findings.title: SAST: Other (36 instances)
findings.description: Semgrep static analysis detected 36 ERROR-severity other issues in /home/ss/code/DVWA. Example: Make sure comparisons involving md5 values are strict (use `===` not `==`) to avoid type juggling issues

findings.title: SAST: Sqli (14 instances)
findings.description: Semgrep static analysis detected 14 ERROR-severity sqli issues in /home/ss/code/DVWA. Example: User data flows into this manually-constructed SQL string. User data can be safely inserted into SQL strings using prepared statements or an object-relational mapper (ORM). Manually-constructed SQL strings is a possible indicator of SQL injection, which could let an attacker steal or manipulate data
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: SAST: Xss (1 instance)
findings.description: Semgrep static analysis detected 1 ERROR-severity xss issue in /home/ss/code/DVWA. Example: `Echo`ing user input risks cross-site scripting vulnerability. You should use `htmlentities()` when showing data to users.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: Hardcoded Secrets in Source Code (2 detected)
findings.description: Secret scanning tools found 2 credentials embedded in /home/ss/code/DVWA (2 by Gitleaks, 0 by TruffleHog). Exposed secrets enable direct unauthorized access to connected services.

tool_executions.output: Elevated 3 finding group(s) from SAST analysis

misconfigurations.title: Image user should not be 'root'
misconfigurations.description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.
misconfigurations.message: Specify at least 1 USER command in Dockerfile with non-root user as argument
misconfigurations.resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile

misconfigurations.title: 'RUN cd ...' to change directory
misconfigurations.description: Use WORKDIR instead of proliferating instructions like 'RUN cd … && do-something', which are hard to read, troubleshoot, and maintain.
misconfigurations.message: RUN should not be used to change directory: 'cd /var/www/html/vulnerabilities/api  && composer install'. Use 'WORKDIR' statement instead.

misconfigurations.title: 'apt-get' missing '--no-install-recommends'
misconfigurations.description: 'apt-get' install should use '--no-install-recommends' to minimize image size.
misconfigurations.message: '--no-install-recommends' flag is missed: 'apt-get update  && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive  && apt-get install -y zlib1g-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev libfreetype6-dev iputils-ping git  && apt-get clean -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*  && docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-jpeg --with-freetype  && a2enmod rewrite  && docker-php-ext-install gd mysqli pdo pdo_mysql'

findings.message: User data flows into this manually-constructed SQL string. User data can be safely inserted into SQL strings using prepared statements or an object-relational mapper (ORM). Manually-constructed SQL strings is a possible indicator of SQL injection, which could let an attacker steal or manipulate data from the database. Instead, use prepared statements (`$mysqli->prepare("INSERT INTO test(id, label) VALUES (?, ?)");`) or a safe library.
cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.message: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.
cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

findings.message: RegExp() called with a `f` function argument, this might allow an attacker to cause a Regular Expression Denial-of-Service (ReDoS) within your application as RegExP blocks the main thread. For this reason, it is recommended to use hardcoded regexes instead. If your regex is run on user-controlled input, consider performing input validation or use a regex checking/sanitization library such as https://www.npmjs.com/package/recheck to verify that the regex does not appear vulnerable to ReDoS.
cwe: CWE-1333: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity

findings.message: User data flows into this manually-constructed SQL string. User data can be safely inserted into SQL strings using prepared statements or an object-relational mapper (ORM). Manually-constructed SQL strings is a possible indicator of SQL injection, which could let an attacker steal or manipulate data from the database. Instead, use prepared statements (`$mysqli->prepare("INSERT INTO test(id, label) VALUES (?, ?)");`) or a safe library.
cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
